fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 32-year-old African American female with schizoaffective disorder for check-in.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Hill presents appearing upbeat. Reports she stopped taking her cariprazine about 3 weeks ago because the copay increased and she's been waiting for her patient assistance application to process. Has missed approximately 20 doses. Feels mostly okay but did have a few days of feeling more anxious and briefly wondered if her coworkers were judging her outfits. No hallucinations. Sleep 6-7 hours. Appetite good, weight 152 lbs. Works as retail associate. Mood 'okay considering' rated 6/10. Lives with boyfriend. No substance use. Denies SI/HI. (Assessed by Dr. Yolanda Martinez, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Brief social anxiety, mild paranoid concerns

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective disorder, stable with recent medication interruption due to cost.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living with boyfriend; employed in retail; financial barriers to medication access.
Assessment_and_Plan: Addressed 3-week medication gap due to cost barriers.
Mild symptoms emerging, will monitor closely.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Yolanda Martinez, MD, Psychiatrist — Affordable Care Clinic, 1650 Community Health Way, Phoenix, AZ 85004, Phone: (555) 447-6621, Fax: (555) 447-6622
